body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: clan template + memory domain ownership
body.description:
**What changed:** Igor becomes an instance of a clan template; Granny and Vetinari declared as memory domain owners for builds and task management respectively; clan = all-agent shared layer.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Template update governance gap — if the Igor template changes (personality, baseline memories), existing Igor instances get a divergent identity silently
- Memory domain ownership creates turf — Granny and Vetinari refuse bus requests from other agents for build/task data even for legitimate read queries
- Clan-as-all-agents blurs the local/global distinction — agents start treating local-shared as 'clan' and write cross-instance data there
- A second Igor instance boots but has no clan-merge conflict resolution — two Igors modify the same clan memory and last-write-wins

**Signals to watch:**
- Igor template version field in profile (should match across instances)
- Bus MEMORY_REQUEST denials (Granny refusing Vetinari = probably wrong)
- Local DB growing with data tagged 'clan' that belongs in global
- Palace: look for duplicate/conflicting clan memory entries across Igor instances

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Observation ticket — done when each predicted effect is annotated
